task_description: Plan a 21-day multi-city trip to Tokyo, Paris, and Barcelona with 7 days in each destination

attributes.name: notes
attributes.value: Multi-city European and Asian adventure. Focus on culture, food, and architecture.

attributes.name: safety_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Tokyo is one of the world's safest major cities with extremely low crime rates, efficient police presence, well-lit streets at all hours, and excellent emergency services. Solo travelers and families feel very secure day and night.

attributes.name: culture_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Tokyo offers an unparalleled blend of ancient traditions and cutting-edge modernity. From centuries-old temples and tea ceremonies to anime culture and robot restaurants, the city provides deep cultural immersion with respect for heritage and innovation.

attributes.name: food_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Tokyo has more Michelin stars than any other city globally. From $3 convenience store meals to world-class sushi at Tsukiji, ramen shops, izakayas, and kaiseki dining, the quality and variety are exceptional at every price point.

value.name: Senso-ji Temple
value.description: Tokyo's oldest Buddhist temple in Asakusa, featuring the iconic Thunder Gate and vibrant Nakamise shopping street

value.name: Shibuya Crossing
value.description: World's busiest pedestrian crossing, epitome of Tokyo's organized chaos and urban energy

value.name: Meiji Shrine
value.description: Serene Shinto shrine surrounded by forest in the heart of the city, dedicated to Emperor Meiji

value.name: Tokyo Skytree
value.description: World's tallest tower at 634m, offering panoramic views of Tokyo and Mount Fuji on clear days

value.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
value.description: Famous fish market with fresh sushi, street food, and authentic Japanese culinary experiences

value.name: Harajuku & Takeshita Street
value.description: Youth fashion capital with quirky shops, kawaii culture, and trendy cafes

value.name: Imperial Palace East Gardens
value.description: Beautiful traditional Japanese gardens on the grounds of the Emperor's residence

value.name: TeamLab Borderless
value.description: Immersive digital art museum with stunning interactive light installations

attributes.name: safety_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Paris is generally safe for tourists but requires awareness of pickpockets in tourist areas like the Eiffel Tower, metro stations, and Montmartre. Avoid displaying valuables and stay vigilant in crowded areas. Most neighborhoods are safe, though some suburbs should be avoided at night.

attributes.name: culture_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Paris is the epitome of art, culture, and sophistication. Home to the Louvre, Musée d'Orsay, Notre-Dame, and countless galleries, the city shaped Western art and philosophy. From café culture to haute couture, every street corner breathes history and elegance.

attributes.name: food_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Paris is the world capital of gastronomy with legendary bistros, patisseries, fromageries, and Michelin-starred restaurants. From fresh croissants and perfect baguettes to coq au vin and escargot, French cuisine reaches its pinnacle here. Wine culture is exceptional.

value.name: Eiffel Tower
value.description: Iconic 330m iron lattice tower, symbol of Paris and must-see landmark with stunning city views

value.name: Louvre Museum
value.description: World's largest art museum housing the Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo, and 38,000 masterpieces

value.name: Notre-Dame Cathedral
value.description: Medieval Gothic cathedral on Île de la Cité, currently under restoration after 2019 fire

value.name: Arc de Triomphe
value.description: Monumental arch honoring French military victories, at the center of Place Charles de Gaulle

value.name: Sacré-Cœur Basilica
value.description: White-domed basilica atop Montmartre hill with panoramic views and bohemian atmosphere

value.name: Musée d'Orsay
value.description: Impressionist art museum in a stunning Beaux-Arts railway station, featuring Monet, Van Gogh, Renoir

value.name: Champs-Élysées
value.description: Famous avenue lined with luxury shops, cafés, and theaters leading to the Arc de Triomphe

value.name: Versailles Palace
value.description: Opulent royal château with Hall of Mirrors and magnificent gardens, 30 minutes from Paris

attributes.name: best_time_to_visit
attributes.value: April-June or September-October (mild weather, fewer crowds)

attributes.name: language
attributes.value: French (English widely spoken in tourist areas)

attributes.name: safety_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Barcelona is generally safe but has issues with pickpocketing, especially on Las Ramblas, metro, and beach areas. Tourists should watch belongings carefully. Violent crime is rare. The Gothic Quarter can feel sketchy late at night. Overall, standard urban precautions ensure a safe visit.

attributes.name: culture_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Barcelona is a cultural powerhouse with Gaudí's architectural masterpieces, Picasso's legacy, vibrant Catalan identity, and Mediterranean lifestyle. The city blends Gothic history with modernist innovation, flamenco with contemporary art, and maintains strong traditions while embracing creativity.

attributes.name: food_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Barcelona excels with tapas culture, fresh seafood, paella, jamón ibérico, and innovative Catalan cuisine. The city has numerous Michelin-starred restaurants alongside authentic bodegas. Markets like La Boqueria offer incredible fresh produce. Wine and vermouth culture is exceptional.

value.name: Sagrada Família
value.description: Gaudí's unfinished masterpiece basilica, Barcelona's most iconic landmark with stunning modernist architecture

value.name: Park Güell
value.description: Whimsical public park designed by Gaudí with colorful mosaics, serpentine benches, and city views

value.name: Las Ramblas
value.description: Famous tree-lined pedestrian boulevard with street performers, markets, and vibrant atmosphere

value.name: Casa Batlló
value.description: Gaudí's fantastical modernist building with organic shapes and colorful facade on Passeig de Gràcia

value.name: Gothic Quarter (Barri Gòtic)
value.description: Medieval neighborhood with narrow streets, Roman ruins, and historic Barcelona Cathedral

value.name: La Boqueria Market
value.description: Vibrant food market on Las Ramblas with fresh produce, seafood, jamón, and local delicacies

value.name: Barceloneta Beach
value.description: Popular urban beach with golden sand, Mediterranean swimming, and beachfront restaurants

value.name: Camp Nou
value.description: FC Barcelona's legendary football stadium, Europe's largest, offering stadium tours and museum

attributes.name: best_time_to_visit
attributes.value: May-June or September-October (warm weather, beach season, fewer tourists)

attributes.name: language
attributes.value: Spanish & Catalan (English common in tourist areas)

value.task: Get JR Pass for Tokyo transport
Download offline maps and translation apps
Exchange currency or get travel card
Pack appropriate clothing for all climates

attributes.name: special_events
attributes.value: Cherry Blossoms (late March-early April), Fall Foliage (November)

attributes.name: weather_notes
attributes.value: Spring offers cherry blossoms and mild weather. Summer is hot, humid with typhoon risk. Fall has comfortable temps and beautiful foliage. Winter is cold but dry with fewer crowds.

attributes.name: rainfall_level
attributes.value: Moderate year-round, slightly higher in May-June

attributes.name: special_events
attributes.value: Paris Fashion Week (Feb/Mar & Sep/Oct), Bastille Day (July 14), Christmas Markets (Dec)

attributes.name: weather_notes
attributes.value: Spring and fall offer pleasant weather with fewer crowds. Summer is warm and lively but crowded. Winter is cold and gray but romantic with holiday decorations. Rain is possible year-round, so pack an umbrella.

attributes.name: rainfall_level
attributes.value: Low, mainly in September-October and spring

attributes.name: special_events
attributes.value: La Mercè Festival (Sept), Sant Jordi (April 23), Primavera Sound (May/June), Sónar Festival (June)

attributes.name: weather_notes
attributes.value: Barcelona enjoys a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and warm summers. Late spring and early fall offer ideal beach weather without extreme heat. Summer can be very hot and crowded. Winter is mild and pleasant for sightseeing.

metadata.description: Trip includes Tokyo as first destination
Trip includes Paris as second destination
Trip includes Barcelona as third destination
